person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Thomas Cranmer was probably born about 1455 at his estate of Aslacton Nottinghamshire, the eldest son and heir of John Cranmer and his wife Alice Marshall. He had a younger brother John Cranmer, who went into the church.

He married Agnes Hatfield, probably some time after 1480, and they had a numerous family of three sons and several daughters, the identity of some being in dispute. (See Note #1 below.) 

«b»These children were:«/b»
1.) John - eldest son and heir, b. about 1487, m. (1) Joan Fretchville (2) Margaret Fitzwilliam

2.) Thomas - second son, Archbishop of Canterbury, b. 1489, m. (1) Joan (2) Margarete, d. 21 May 1566

3.) Edmund - youngest son, Archdeacon of Canterbury, b. about 1491, m. Alice Sands, d. 1571

4.) Alice - became a nun 

5.) Dorothy - m. Harold Rossell

6.) Anne (Agnes) - m. Edmund Cartwright

7.) Margaret - named in Thomas's Will

8.) Emmot - named in Thomas's Will

9.) Jane - disputed, possibly m. John Monins

10.) Isabella - disputed, possibly m. Sir ___ Shepey

Thomas died 27 May 1501, and his Will was proved at York on 1 October 1501. It was witnessed by his brother John Cranmer, magister. He left bequests to his wife Agnes, who survived him, annuities of 20s (plus 40s) to his two younger sons, and bequests to two daughters, Margaret and Emmot. His other daughters were not mentioned; it can be assumed that they received their inheritance as marriage portions, or, in the case of Alice, dowry to the nunnery.

Thomas Cranmer was buried in the parish church of Whatton, Nottinghamshire, near Aslacton. His tombstone was a limestone slab with his figure incised and the inscription: Here lies Thomas Cramer, esquire, who died on the 27th day of the month of May in the year of the Lord 1501, may God have mercy on his soul. Amen, There are also incised shields with the ancestral Cranmer arms and the arms of Newmarch, to whom his Aslacton ancestors were related.

«b»Note #1: Daughters«/b»
There seems to have been a tradition that Thomas Cranmer of Aslacton had four daughters. Diarmid MacCullough has added a fifth, not previously on the lists: Alice, who became a nun. All the sources above agree that Dorothy, who married Harold Rossell, and Agnes (or Anne) who married Edmund Cartwright of Ossington, were daughters of Thomas Cranmer; these were prominent local families whose pedigrees include these wives. And the only contemporary source - Thomas's Will - names Margaret and Emmot, who do not appear in the earlier pedigrees.

It is possible that some earlier genealogist, unaware of the contents of the Will, added the names of Jane and Isabella to make up the desired number four. These are found in the 1569/1614 Visitation, and were likely copied by later writers,e.g. Thoroton. But their identification as daughters of Thomas Cranmer is disputed by Pollard and Waters. For Isabelle the lady of Shepey there seems to be no evidence at all. As for Jane, wife of John Monins, according to Waters a Jane Monins did exist who was named in the Will of John Monins, but Waters found no evidence that she was a daughter of Thomas Cranmer. The mother of Monins' children appears to have been a Margaret Alde, of Kent. There is also the evidence of the Will of Edward Whitchurch, who married the widow of Archbishop Thomas Cranmer, which refers to his "sister Moning", but again, contrary to the assertion given in the abstract here, nothing identifies this Moning as a Jane, let alone a Jane Cranmer. The evidence must be considered inconclusive.

«b»Note #2: Dates«/b»
For the 15th century Cranmers of Aslacton, there are two defining dates. First is 1425/6, when Edmund Cranmer, of Aslacton, and Isabell his Wife, 4 H. 6. claimed against William de Sibthorp, Esquire, two Mess. one Toft, twenty-six Acres of Land, and two of Meadow, in Aslacton. This places the marriage of Edmund Cranmer and Isabelle Aslacton as before this date, and allows us to estimate their births as well as the births of their son and grandchildren.

The other date is the 27 May 1501 death of Thomas Cranmer, which also figures into estimating the birthdates of his children, particularly the daughters, at least two of whom were probably married by that time.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Cranmer於大約1455年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾阿斯洛可頓時，他的父親John Cranmer32歲，母親Alice Marshall27歲。他於1488年與Agnes Hatfielde在英國諾廷加姆爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1501年5月27日在他的家鄉享年47歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾華亞頓。

nameMeanings.name: Cranmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Cranmer Green in Walsham le Willows (Suffolk), and perhaps also from other places similarly named, such as Cranmer Hall in Sculthorpe (Norfolk), Cranmore in Shalfleet (Isle of Wight), Cranmer Bottom in Selborne and Cranmore in Aldershot (both Hampshire), and East and West Cranmore (Somerset), all named from Old English cran ‘crane’ + mere ‘lake, pool’ or mōr ‘moor, marshy ground’. See also Cranmore , with which there may have been some confusion.

variants.name: Cranmore
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Worcestershire): variant of   Cranmer  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Cranmer was born about 1455, in Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, John Cranmer, was 32 and his mother, Alice Marshall, was 27. He married Agnes Hatfielde in 1488, in Nottinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 27 May 1501, in his hometown, at the age of 47, and was buried in Whatton, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Cranmer est né environ 1455, à Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John Cranmer, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Alice Marshall, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Hatfielde en 1488, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 27 mai 1501, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Whatton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Cranmer um 1455 in Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Cranmer 32 und seine Mutter Alice Marshall 27. Er hat 1488 in Nottinghamshire, England Agnes Hatfielde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 27. Mai 1501 im Alter von 47 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Whatton, Nottinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Cranmer è nato il circa 1455 nel Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, England, il suo padre, John Cranmer, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Alice Marshall, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Agnes Hatfielde nel 1488, nel Nottinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 27 maggio 1501, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Whatton, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Cranmerが約1455年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアアスロクトンで生まれた時，父John Cranmerは32歳，母Alice Marshallは27歳であった。1488年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでAgnes Hatfieldeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1501年5月27日に故郷で47歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアウアットオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Cranmer(이)가 대략 1455년 영국 노틴그함실르 아슬록턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Cranmer(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Marshall(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1488년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 Agnes Hatfielde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1501년 5월 27일 그의 고향에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 화엇에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Cranmer nasceu aproximadamente 1455, em Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Cranmer, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Alice Marshall, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes Hatfielde em 1488, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas. Ele faleceu em 27 de maio de 1501, em sua cidade natal, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Whatton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Cranmer родился около 1455 г. в местности аслокктон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Cranmer было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Marshall, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Hatfielde в 1488 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Он умер 27 мая 1501 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности уаттон, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Cranmer nació aproximadamente 1455, en Aslockton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Cranmer, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Alice Marshall, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Agnes Hatfielde en 1488, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 27 de mayo de 1501, en su pueblo, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Whatton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
